content.problemStatement:
In Java, **Strings are immutable**, meaning once a string object is created, its value cannot be changed. Any modification (like concatenation or replacement) results in the creation of a new string object. This ensures security, thread-safety, and memory efficiency through string interning, where identical strings are stored in a single memory location.

---

Example:
```java
String text = "Hello";
text.concat(" World");
System.out.println(text); // Output: Hello (original string remains unchanged)
```
